Summary: President Trump extended a Jones Act waiver for 90 days to ease oil, fuel, and fertilizer transport between US ports, aiming to curb rising energy costs linked to the war with Iran. The waiver, originally granted for 60 days in March, is part of broader efforts to address fuel price spikes before midterm elections, though analysts say its impact on lowering consumer prices is modest. The Jones Act requires goods shipped between US ports to be carried on US-flagged vessels, and its waiver has drawn criticism from supporters of the law.

Summary: A US appeals court ruled that President Trump's ban on asylum applications is unlawful, stating that existing laws grant individuals the right to seek asylum at the border. The three-judge panel found that Trump's January 2025 proclamation exceeded executive authority by circumventing the Immigration and Nationality Act. The White House plans to appeal the decision, which blocks the order but leaves its immediate impact unclear.

The article explains a growing gap between physical spot prices (like Dated Brent) and futures prices, reflecting a mismatch between supply perceptions and reality due to Iran's effective shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz. This disruption, allowing only minimal vessel transit, is causing one of the biggest energy shocks in history.

The astronauts underwent a high-speed re-entry and parachute landing after their 10-day voyage. The mission, which set several historic firsts, is seen as a critical test flight for future lunar landing plans.

Summary: Pakistan is facing a looming gas shortage crisis after starting the year with an LNG surplus. This shift occurred after the US-Israel war on Iran led to attacks on key gas facilities in Qatar and Iran, disrupting supplies through the Strait of Hormuz. As a result, Qatar halted LNG production and declared force majeure, severely impacting Pakistan, which relies heavily on imports from Qatar and the UAE with no emergency reserves.

[2] How China’s ‘teapot’ refineries are cushioning it from Iran war oil crisis | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
Published: 2026-04-03T05:57:07Z
URL: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/3/how-chinas-teapot-refineries-are-cushioning-it-from-iran-war-oil-crisis
Summary: China's small independent 'teapot' refineries are helping cushion the country from the oil crisis caused by the war on Iran and the paralysis of the Strait of Hormuz. These refineries had stockpiled oil from countries like Iran, Russia, and Venezuela, capitalizing on cheap oil due to sanctions. While China's oil supply is not entirely immune, its seaborne crude imports in March remained broadly in line with 2025 averages, though most arrivals were loaded before the conflict began.

He received at least 293 votes, passing the majority threshold, following a lopsided election won by an army-backed party. His ascent to the presidency, after a military leadership reshuffle, is seen as a strategic move to consolidate power under a nominally civilian government while a civil war continues to rage.

The farmers' case relates to the companies being among major industrial producers of historical carbon emissions.

Published: 2026-03-19T23:54:22Z

Summary: The US Commission of Fine Arts, composed of Trump appointees, unanimously approved plans for a commemorative gold coin featuring a portrait of Donald Trump. The coin may sidestep federal law prohibiting living presidents on currency, as it is commemorative and not for circulation. The Trump administration has also advanced plans to put the president's face on a $1 coin, which critics denounce as unlawful and inappropriate for a democracy.
